title: horsfordia alata
intro: Horsfordia alata, also known as Indian spurge, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Horsfordia alata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Horsfordia alata is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or paste, and is also used topically in the form of creams and ointments.
